_comment: Shared parity fixture for ffmpeg Raw-mode arg construction. `expected` = the FROZEN current TS output of src/lib/shellArgs.ts (parseCommandArgs) + src/lib/commandBuilder.ts (buildCommandArgs); the TS reference test (tests/unit/rawArgs.parity.test.ts) asserts it. The Rust test (src-tauri/src/ffmpeg/raw_args.rs) asserts `rustExpected` when present, else `expected`. `rustExpected` is set in two situations: (1) intentional, out-of-scope divergence where Rust does a clean literal str::replace and does NOT emulate JS String.replace's $-pattern interpretation (the $ cases); (2) the deliberate SPACE FIX, where Rust tokenizes the template BEFORE substitution so paths with spaces stay a single arg. See each case's `note`.

tokenizer.name: double quotes containing a single quote
adjacent quoted segment merges into neighbor
special chars unquoted are kept literally
windows path with space, UNQUOTED -> splits
tokenizer.note: Tokenizer EN ISOLATION: une chaîne brute non quotée avec espace se découpe — comportement correct et INCHANGÉ. Le fix des espaces vit dans build_raw_args (cas pipeline 'windows paths WITH spaces'), qui tokenise le template AVANT de substituer les chemins.

tokenizer.name: windows path with space, quoted -> single token
unclosed quote keeps content, drops quote

buildRawArgs.name: windows paths WITH spaces, unquoted template -> splits (current behavior)
buildRawArgs.note: FIX (pas portage). `expected` = ancien comportement TS cassé (le chemin se découpe sur l'espace). `rustExpected` = sortie corrigée : Rust tokenise le template AVANT substitution, donc le chemin avec espaces reste un seul argument.

buildRawArgs.name: gif filter graph with special chars
user manually quotes placeholders -> spaces preserved
path literally containing {output} (sequential replace also substitutes it)
buildRawArgs.note: Sequential replace: {input} first, then {output} globally — so the {output} injected by the input path is ALSO substituted. Rust must replicate input-then-output sequential replace to match.

buildRawArgs.name: plain dollar in path (no special pattern) -> literal
DOLLAR-AMPERSAND in input path (JS replace interprets $&)
buildRawArgs.note: DIVERGENCE VOLONTAIRE (hors périmètre). JS String.replace interprète $& comme la sous-chaîne capturée '{input}'. Rust fait un str::replace littéral — comportement voulu. Le résultat TS est un artefact accidentel de JS, pas une référence à préserver.

buildRawArgs.name: DOUBLE-DOLLAR in input path (JS replace collapses $$ -> $)
buildRawArgs.note: DIVERGENCE VOLONTAIRE (hors périmètre). JS réduit $$ à un seul $. Rust littéral garde $$ — comportement voulu.

buildRawArgs.name: DOLLAR-AMPERSAND in output path (JS replace interprets $&)
buildRawArgs.note: DIVERGENCE VOLONTAIRE (hors périmètre). Même interprétation $& côté output. Rust littéral — comportement voulu.

buildRawArgs.name: bare template is just the input token
